title: stylidium exappendiculatum
intro: Stylidium exappendiculatum, also known as the triggerplant, is a small to medium-sized plant species that belongs to the family Stylidiaceae and is native to eastern Australia. The medicinal properties of the triggerplant are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are beneficial for treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Stylidium exappendiculatum include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and combat infections. Herbal preparations of the triggerplant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin irritations and respiratory problems.
